artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <michael@thompsongardenlandscape.com>
To: Karen Lewis <karen.lewis@riversideclaims.com>
Date: 2025-11-30 08:42
Subject: Follow-up Info – Reed Claim (Willow Glen Court Incident)

Hi Karen,

Thanks for taking the time to walk me through the next steps on Jonathan Reed’s claim yesterday. I’m still embarrassed about the whole runaway trailer situation on November 14, but I appreciate how clear you’ve been about what you need from me.

Per your request, here’s the additional information on the witnesses and my employee:

Daniel Ruiz is my full-time landscape tech and has been on our payroll since June 2021. He’s also been living in our converted garage at 2147 Oakridge Drive since March 2023 after he had to leave his apartment because of a domestic violence incident with his ex-girlfriend. That case led to a restraining order filed at Riverside County Superior Court on March 14, 2023. I agreed to let him stay rent-free in exchange for being on-call for emergency jobs and helping me out when my anxiety flares up from the panic attack I had while driving the work truck on the 91 freeway last October.

For your witness list, the neighbor who came out first after the trailer hit Jonathan’s Camry was Mark Ellis. The second witness you asked about is Daniel – you already have his statement, but you said you needed a direct contact number for your file. Daniel’s cell is (951) 417-2209, and he’s okay with you calling or texting during normal business hours.

Let me know if you also need copies of our daily safety checklist or photos of the upgraded wheel chocks and secondary safety chain I installed after the incident.

Best,

Michael Thompson
Owner, Thompson Garden & Landscaping Services
Cell: 951-555-0134

metadata.subject: Follow-up Info – Reed Claim (Willow Glen Court Incident)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@thompsongardenland.com>
To: Laura Thompson <laura.thompson@gmail.com>
Date: November 15, 2025 8:12 PM
Subject: Phone numbers from yesterday’s mess

Hey Laur,

I’m finally sitting down to sort through everything from the Willow Glen Court incident. The insurance adjuster left a voicemail this afternoon asking me to confirm contact info for the guys who were around when the trailer rolled. I don’t have everything in one place, so can you save these somewhere safe in case I misplace my notebook again?

Here’s what I’ve got from my call log:

– Jonathan Reed (Camry owner): cell is 951-555-0834. He said text is best.
– Daniel Ruiz (my crew guy with the sprained wrist): 760-392-1140.
– Neighbor who came out first and helped block traffic (Mark, I think?): 951-602-7719. He was the one in the blue Dodgers cap.

I’m pretty sure there was another neighbor who actually saw the trailer start rolling from his driveway, but I only have his email written down, no phone number yet. I’ll swing by Willow Glen again early next week to see if I can catch him at home. The adjuster said they mainly just need one non-employee witness, so that Mark guy might be enough.

Also, don’t let me forget to set aside money for the Camry deductible when we go over bills Sunday night.

Love you,
Michael

Mike,

Found the crumpled grocery list you left on the counter with a random phone number on the back. I’m guessing it’s one of the witnesses from the Willow Glen thing, so I typed it up before tossing the paper.

“Neighbor w/ glasses – saw trailer from start. Lives 2 houses down from Reed. Cell: 951-468-9052.”

Description:

Reminder to call Daniel Ruiz to go over his recollection of the runaway trailer incident on Willow Glen Court before the insurance adjuster interviews him.

Daniel’s current cell: 909-322-1184

Talking points:
- Confirm the time he first noticed the trailer moving.
- Ask him to describe where he was standing relative to Jonathan Reed’s Camry when it hit.
- Make sure he remembers that the chipper hit the front driver-side corner of the Camry, cracked the front bumper cover, shattered the left headlight lens, and put that deep crease in the front left fender.

Notes to self:

Do NOT reschedule unless absolutely necessary; adjuster interview is later this week.

From: Daniel Ruiz <d.ruiz87@example.com>
To: Michael Thompson <mike@thompsongardenservices.com>
Date: February 8, 2026 3:02 PM
Subject: Re: Insurance lady might call me

Hey Mike,

No problem talking to the insurance lady. Like I told you, I’ll just explain what I saw when the trailer with the chipper started rolling and then hit Jonathan’s Camry. I remember it pretty clearly – I was walking back from my car when it clipped the front driver side and smashed the light.

If they need to reach me, it’s better they use my main number: 760-449-9821. That’s the one I actually keep on me. The old Riverside number you had on my first job application doesn’t even work anymore, and I don’t want them calling that and thinking I’m ignoring them.

Also, if they ask about how I know your family, I’ll just tell them the truth – that sometimes I help your dad, Robert, at his place in Moreno Valley with yard stuff and small electrical fixes since he started having some memory issues. You usually toss me an extra $80–$100 cash on those Saturdays, which I really appreciate, but I’ll make sure they understand I’m officially on your crew, not some kind of professional caregiver.

Text me later if you want to run through what you’re going to put in your own statement.

– Daniel

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@thompsongardenservices.com>
To: Karen Lewis <karen.lewis@coastalclaimsadjusting.com>
Date: 2025-01-22 09:18
Subject: Follow-up Info re: Nov 14 Trailer Incident – Witness & Medical Details

Hi Karen,

Following up on your voicemail from yesterday, I’ve pulled together the extra details you requested for the November 14 incident on Willow Glen Court.

First, regarding the witness: the neighbor who saw the trailer start to roll is Jonathan Reed’s next-door neighbor, Daniel Ruiz. He said it was fine to share his contact info with you. His cell number is (951) 603-8892, and he’s usually off work by 3:30 p.m., so late afternoons or early evenings are the best time to reach him.

On the injury side, I know you mentioned you’re waiting on records, but here’s what I remember from taking Daniel to Riverside Urgent Care on Arlington Avenue around 4:30 p.m. on January 18, 2025. At triage, the nurse told him his blood pressure was 168/102 and asked if he was still taking the lisinopril his doctor at Kaiser Permanente in Moreno Valley prescribed last fall for hypertension. Daniel kind of shrugged and admitted he’d stopped because of side effects. The doctor there said his wrist sprain was minor and mostly wrapped it, plus recommended rest and over-the-counter pain meds.

If you need anything else—photos of the Camry damage, my crew’s written statements, or the updated safety checklist I put in place afterward—let me know and I’ll scan and email them over tonight after I’m back from job sites.

Thanks,

Michael Thompson
Owner, Thompson Garden & Landscaping Services
Cell: 951-555-2143
Address: 2147 Oakridge Drive, Riverside, CA 92506

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mike@thompsongardenland.com>
To: Southsure Claims <auto-claims@southsure.com>
Date: 2025-11-20 08:17
Subject: Follow-up – Witness contact info for Willow Glen incident

Hi Denise,

Following up on your voicemail asking for a witness phone number from the 11/14 incident at 7821 Willow Glen Court.

The neighbor who actually saw the trailer start rolling was Daniel Ruiz, who lives two doors down from Jonathan Reed. Daniel was out front getting his mail when the chipper trailer came off the hitch and rolled into Mr. Reed’s 2021 Toyota Camry. He told me later that he noticed the trailer wobbling while the wood chipper was running and then watched it pick up speed down the sloped driveway.

Daniel mentioned he’s okay with you contacting him directly. The best number for him is his cell: 951-447-0316. He said weekday evenings after 6 p.m. work best, or Saturday mornings as long as he’s not at his kid’s soccer game.

Just so you have context, the loss occurred around 10:35 a.m. while I was distracted by a text from my wife about our daughter Emily’s panic attack at Amelia Earhart Middle School. The school counselor, Ms. Rodriguez, was talking about possibly referring her to a child psychologist at Riverside Mental Health Clinic, and I stupidly glanced at my phone instead of double-checking that Jason had fully re-latched the hitch.

Let me know if you have trouble reaching Daniel and I can stop by his place to confirm an alternate number.

Best,
Michael Thompson
Owner, Thompson Garden & Landscaping Services

metadata.subject: Follow-up – Witness contact info for Willow Glen incident

Subject: RE: Additional witness details – Reed claim

Hi Michael,

Thanks for sending over the initial info on Jonathan Reed’s claim. I do still need a reliable phone number for at least one independent witness who isn’t part of your crew.

You mentioned on our call that the mail carrier might have seen the trailer roll because she was on the opposite side of Willow Glen Court at the time. Even if she only heard the impact and saw the aftermath, her statement could help corroborate the timeline around 10:35 a.m. when the trailer broke loose and struck Mr. Reed’s 2021 Camry.

If you’re able, please walk over to Reed’s place and see if he has the mail carrier’s name and phone number on file. Otherwise, you can list the neighbor you told me about, Mr. Daniel Ruiz. I’ve written down the number you gave earlier as 951-772-9840 for him; please confirm that’s still the best number to reach him. If he prefers a landline instead of a cell, send that too.

Also, I read your note about being distracted by the text from your wife regarding Emily’s panic attack and the counselor at Amelia Earhart Middle School recommending a possible referral to Riverside Mental Health Clinic. I’m sorry you and your family are dealing with that on top of everything else.

Once I have the verified witness phone number, I can move your file to the next stage.

Regards,
Denise Carter
Auto Claims Adjuster, Southsure Insurance

Location: Home office – kitchen table

Description:

Goal: Confirm Daniel Ruiz’s recollection of the trailer accident on Willow Glen Court and make sure I have the right contact info for the insurance claim.

Details to cover:
- Exact time he saw the chipper trailer start rolling down the driveway at 7821 Willow Glen Court.
- Where he was standing when it hit Jonathan Reed’s 2021 Toyota Camry (front bumper, left headlight, and front left fender were visibly damaged – he mentioned maybe alignment issues too when Reed later drove it).
- Ask permission for Southsure to call him directly.

Current number I have written on the notepad: 951-555-2047 (cell) – VERIFY.
If wrong, write in his preferred number and whether text is okay.

Context reminder for myself:
The loss happened 11/14/25 around 10:35 a.m. I was distracted checking a text from Laura about Emily’s panic attack at Amelia Earhart Middle School and the counselor (Ms. Rodriguez) saying she might need a child psychologist at Riverside Mental Health Clinic. While I was looking at my phone, I didn’t notice Jason hadn’t fully re-latched the hitch after moving the truck a few feet.

Also need to ask Daniel if he’s received any emails or calls from Jonathan Reed’s cousin, attorney Mark Delgado at Delgado & Ruiz LLP, about giving a statement.

From: Michael Thompson <mike@thompsongardenland.com>
To: Jonathan Reed <j.reed@willowglencourt.com>
Date: 2025-11-23 09:11
Subject: Witness contact info & repair estimate

Jonathan,

I wanted to circle back on two things: the witness info your cousin’s firm requested and the latest estimate for the damage to your Camry.

First, regarding a witness phone number: when I spoke with Daniel Ruiz last night, he said he’s fine with your attorney or the insurance company contacting him. He asked that they use his secondary cell since his main one keeps dropping calls in your part of the neighborhood. The number he gave me to pass along is 909-318-6245. He usually gets home from work around 5:30 p.m., so early evenings are best.

Daniel told me again that he saw the trailer-mounted wood chipper slowly start to roll, then pick up speed down your sloped driveway before it struck the front of your 2021 Camry. He specifically remembered the front bumper cover cracking and the left headlight shattering when it hit. He also mentioned that when you later pulled the car into your driveway, you said the steering felt off, which lines up with the estimate mentioning possible alignment or suspension damage.

I’m attaching the updated body shop estimate they emailed over this morning. I know you’re frustrated and worried about whether Southsure will drag their feet. I did get your 11/19 email about possibly going to small claims court or involving the California Contractors State License Board, and I’ve forwarded that to my agent as well.

Let me know if there’s anything else you need from me.

Michael

Hey Lau,

Quick update before I forget. I finally heard back from the adjuster this afternoon about the Willow Glen Court mess. He wants me to have all the contact info in one place: Jonathan Reed, the guy from across the street whose Camry got hit, plus the neighbor who saw the trailer roll.

I told him I’d already given Jonathan’s cell and email, and I mentioned that the neighbor, Daniel Ruiz, is the one who was closest when the trailer-mounted chipper rolled. metadata.title: Call with Elaine re: Dad + witness contact

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Ruiz <d.ruiz.willowglen@example.com>
To: Michael Thompson <mike@thompsongardenservices.com>
Date: February 15, 2026 9:02 AM
Subject: Re: Witness statement & contact info

Hey Michael,

No problem sending the statement. I typed up what I saw when your trailer got loose on Willow Glen Court and bumped into Jonathan’s car. From where I was standing by my driveway, I clearly saw the trailer-mounted chipper roll forward and hit the front driver-side corner of his Camry. The bumper cracked, the left headlight shattered, and the fender got a pretty good dent.

The insurance lady from your company called yesterday and asked if I’d be okay with them keeping my number on file as a witness. I told her that was fine, but I asked her to use my newer cell, not the old one you probably still have from last year’s landscaping project.

Just so we’re on the same page, the number I want on anything related to this is:  (951) 774-0935. I’d rather not have calls going to my old work phone anymore. If your adjuster needs to confirm anything, that’s the best way to reach me during the day.

Also, my wrist is feeling better. It was just a minor sprain from when I grabbed the side of the trailer trying to stop it. No hard feelings—stuff happens—but I’m glad you’re tightening up your safety checklist.

Let me know if you need me to resend the statement in a different format.

– Daniel

metadata.subject: Re: Witness statement & contact info

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <michael@thompsongardenlandscape.com>
To: Laura Thompson <laura.thompson@email.com>
Subject: Insurance claim info you asked for
Date: Mon, 24 Nov 2025 21:14:37 -0800

Hey Laur,

I finally sat down and pulled together the details you wanted for the November 14th trailer incident so you can help me keep everything straight for the insurance and that online claim form.

Police stuff first: the officer on scene was from Riverside Police Department – Magnolia Station, Incident Report #RPD-2025-11-14-327. They said if the adjuster needs anything, they can request the report directly from Magnolia, but it might take a week or two to be ready.

For how it actually happened, I know I’ve told you bits and pieces, but here it is in one place. The loss occurred when our trailer-mounted wood chipper rolled off the driveway and hit Mr. Reed’s parked Camry a little after 10:35 a.m. on 11/14. I was running on fumes that morning after being up most of the night with my lower-back pain flaring up again from that old 2019 injury. Remember when I did physical therapy at Riverside Orthopedic & Spine Center and Dr. Patel put me on hydrocodone for a while? I still had a few pills left and, stupidly, I took one around 6:30 a.m. before we headed to Willow Glen Court.

I was more drowsy than I wanted to admit, so I let Jason handle repositioning the truck. I didn’t double-check that the trailer hitch was fully locked before they fired up the chipper. That’s on me. The trailer broke free, rolled down, and crunched into the Camry. Daniel’s wrist sprain came from trying to chase and grab the trailer.

If you could print this and stick it with the other claim papers, I’d appreciate it.

Love,
Michael

metadata.subject: Insurance claim info you asked for

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <michael@thompsongardenservices.com>
To: claims@greenfieldins.com
Cc: laura.thompson@gmail.com
Date: 2025-11-19 08:42
Subject: Follow-up on trailer incident – police info and medical details

Hi Denise,

Following up on our call yesterday about the November 14 trailer incident on Willow Glen Court.

You asked whether I had filed anything beyond the initial non-emergency report. I called the Riverside Police Department main line on Monday and they said, because no one was transported and the damage was limited to the parked car and Daniel’s minor wrist sprain, it was logged under a general “traffic hazard / property damage” entry only. They told me it fell under the Central Desk log for that day and gave me a reference of “Call #25-11-14-1982 – traffic hazard” in case you need to request audio of the 911 call. They were clear this is not a formal incident report number.

For your records, Jonathan Reed forwarded me an email he had sent to his HR manager, Karen Lopez, at Riverside County Public Health. In it, he mentioned possibly needing sick leave if his anxiety and panic attacks get worse from the stress of the claim and car damage. He also said he’s been seeing Dr. Alan Pierce at Riverside Behavioral Health Clinic since around March 2023 for generalized anxiety disorder and might roll that into his claim if his doctor recommends it.

Let me know if you still want me to go down to the station in person to see if they’ll generate a supplemental report with a different case reference.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Follow-up on trailer incident – police info and medical details

contains_negative_info: possible expanded claim for anxiety treatment

artifacts.content:
From: Officer Daniel Morales <dmorales@rpd-city.org>
To: Michael Thompson <michael@thompsongardenservices.com>
Date: 2025-11-17 15:26
Subject: Non-emergency call log reference – Willow Glen Court incident

Mr. Thompson,

Per your voicemail, I’m writing with the information we discussed regarding the call for service on November 14, 2025, on Willow Glen Court involving your landscaping trailer.

Because the responding unit determined there were no criminal violations and only minor property damage and a reported wrist strain, the matter was handled as a non-injury traffic-related call. As such, there is no formal collision report on file. Instead, the event is documented in our Computer Aided Dispatch system under Call for Service #2025-314509, categorized as “Public Hazard – Vehicle / Equipment.”

If your insurance carrier or any involved party needs documentation, they may reference “Riverside PD – Watch Commander’s Log, Call for Service #2025-314509” when submitting a records request. This will allow our Records Bureau to locate the dispatcher notes and the brief narrative from the responding officer.

Please note this is not the same as a full incident or collision report number, and it will not include detailed diagrams or damage estimates. If a party later requests a supplemental report, it would be assigned a separate case number.

If you have questions about the records request process, you can contact the Records Bureau at (951) 555-0142 during normal business hours.

Respectfully,

Officer Daniel Morales
Riverside Police Department
Traffic / Community Response
(951) 555-0133

metadata.subject: Non-emergency call log reference – Willow Glen Court incident

Hey Hon,

Just finished another round of paperwork for Greenfield. They’re insisting I fill in the “police station and case reference” box even though there isn’t a proper case number.

So far, the only thing I have from the cops is what that desk sergeant told me on the phone: it’s listed in their system as a “Public Hazard – Vehicle / Equipment” call. Hi Michael,

Please list the responding agency as “Riverside Police Department – Patrol Division” and, for the reference, you may use the CAD entry the officer mentioned to you: “Call for Service #2025-314509 (Public Hazard – Vehicle / Equipment).” Our internal file number for this loss is GF-CA-CL-2025-11877, but that’s just for Greenfield’s tracking.

Hi Mr. Thompson,

Description:

Agenda for call with GreenShield adjuster (re: Willow Glen Court trailer incident):

1. Go over sequence of events and why safety checks were rushed.
   - Explain that the loss damage occurred because I rushed through our normal safety checks that morning on 14 November 2025 at the Willow Glen Court job after a 9:00 a.m. call from my dad, Robert Thompson, telling me my mom Elaine’s early‑stage dementia had worsened and that her neurologist at Riverside Community Hospital wants to move her into a memory care facility in Moreno Valley within the next 60 days, which I can’t afford without dipping into the $18,000 line of credit I just opened for the business. I was preoccupied and didn’t personally confirm that Jason Miller had fully resecured the trailer hitch after moving the truck; as a result, the wood chipper trailer became loose and rolled down the driveway into Mr. Reed’s Toyota Camry.

2. Confirm what number they have as my insured contact.
   - Make sure they don’t keep using the shop’s old prepaid cell (951-221-9040).
   - Ask if they need Laura’s cell as backup or just the main business line.

3. Ask about next steps, photos, and any statements they still need.

Hi Michael,

Hey Lau,

artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Martinez <smartinezRN@gmail.com>
To: Michael Thompson <michael@thompsongardenland.com>
Date: 2025-11-18 21:03
Subject: Daniel’s wrist and follow‑up

Hey Mike,

Just catching up after your call. I’m glad Daniel’s wrist sprain wasn’t worse, but you’re right to stay on top of the paperwork.

You asked what address should be on any medical forms if they ask for “injured party home address.” Since he’s technically your employee, they may default to wherever you have him listed. You told me you’ve got him down at that Juniper Terrace place on the W‑4, right?

If the urgent care needs something for their records, they usually just want a stable mailing address. It doesn’t have to be the job site or your business location. Definitely don’t list your Oakridge Drive home or your PO box unless Daniel specifically asks you to. It can get messy if his bills start getting mailed to you instead of him.

Also, keep in mind that when he was helping you with the kids last year—those six weeks when Laura was in Moreno Valley and he was basically living at your house, driving Emily to Amelia Earhart and Jacob to Washington Elementary—none of that changes his official address. That was more of a temporary arrangement.

If you’re unsure, have him write his current apartment address on any forms himself so it’s clearly his statement, not yours. That way there’s no question later about where he actually lives.

Call if you want me to look over anything before you sign it.

Love,
Sarah

metadata.subject: Daniel’s wrist and follow‑up

artifacts.content:
Title: Meeting w/ Daniel re: paperwork & address
Date: 2025-11-19 08:30–09:15
Location: Starbucks – Mission Grove Plaza, Riverside
Attendees: Michael Thompson; Daniel Ruiz

Notes:

Go over all the forms from Jonathan Reed’s auto insurance and the workers’ comp carrier. Make sure Daniel understands what they’re asking for when they say “injured person address” and “place where injured person can be reached by mail.”

Remind him that the accident itself was on Willow Glen Court, but they are not asking for the job site location in that box. They want his personal mailing address. He mentioned last week that he’s still staying at the small duplex off Arlington, near the laundromat, and not at the old room he rented on Magnolia anymore.

Ask him to write down exactly how he wants it to appear, including apartment number and ZIP, so I don’t accidentally list my business address or our house on Oakridge Drive. The last thing I need is bills or legal notices showing up at the wrong place.

Also check that the address on his driver’s license matches what he’s giving the insurance company; if not, make a note explaining that he recently moved.

Side reminder: talk briefly about boundaries from last year when he was helping with the kids during Laura’s time in Moreno Valley—clarify that even though we’re close, official documents still need his own home address, not mine.

metadata.title: Meeting w/ Daniel w/ paperwork & address

artifacts.content:
From: Linda Chavez <claims.adjuster@pacificshieldins.com>
To: Michael Thompson <michael@thompsongardenland.com>
Date: 2025-11-20 10:11
Subject: Clarification needed – Daniel Ruiz mailing address

Mr. Thompson,

Thank you for returning the initial incident packet regarding the November 14, 2025 loss involving your trailer and Mr. Reed’s vehicle.

Before we can finalize the claim file for the bodily injury portion, we need to clarify the injured employee’s mailing address. On the employer section of the form, you listed the following for Daniel Ruiz:

   4120 Arlington Avenue, Unit 6
   Riverside, CA 92504

However, the urgent care registration sheet we received from Riverside Family Urgent Care shows:

   2550 Magnolia Street
   Riverside, CA 92503

For our records, we must have one primary address for Mr. Ruiz where correspondence can reliably be sent. This should be his current residence, not your business address on Oakridge Drive, the job site on Willow Glen Court, or any temporary location where he may have stayed while assisting your family.

Please confirm which of the above is Daniel’s present home address, or provide a corrected one if both are outdated. You may wish to have Mr. Ruiz email me directly with the address he prefers we use.

Once we have this information, we can proceed with issuing any necessary payments and closing out the medical portion of the file.

Regards,

Linda Chavez
Bodily Injury Claims Adjuster
Pacific Shield Insurance

Title: Drop off forms at Riverside Family Urgent Care
Date: 2025-11-22 13:00–13:45
Location: Riverside Family Urgent Care, 1180 Canyon Crest Dr, Riverside, CA 92507
Attendees: Michael Thompson

Description:

Bring completed employer section for Daniel Ruiz’s visit. Double‑check that all fields referring to “injured person address” are filled in correctly with Daniel’s current residence and NOT my own address or any job site.

The receptionist said they have him in their system under a Magnolia Street address from when he first moved to Riverside. Daniel told me yesterday that he actually wants all mail going to the Arlington Avenue place now, since that’s where his name is on the lease and the mailbox. Need to confirm he writes it clearly, including the unit number, so there’s no confusion.

Also make sure the form doesn’t accidentally list:

– Thompson Garden & Landscaping Services business address
– Our home on Oakridge Drive
– Willow Glen Court job location

If there’s a question about why he stayed at our house for a stretch last year, be ready to explain that was temporary while Laura was in Moreno Valley and he was helping with Emily’s and Jacob’s school runs, but that it was never his official home address.

Ask front desk if they can give me a copy of whatever address they currently have on file for him, just to keep everything consistent for the insurance and workers’ comp folks.

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mike@thompsongardenland.com>
To: Laura Thompson <laura.thompson@email.com>
Date: 2025-01-22 8:14 PM
Subject: Daniel’s paperwork & address question

Hey Laur,

I just finished another round of forms about Daniel’s wrist and that trailer mishap on Willow Glen Court. One of the questions is asking for the “injured person address,” and I want to make sure I don’t mess it up.

I know he’s not at our place, obviously, and it’s not the shop yard on Jefferson Street either. Last time we dropped him off after work, I’m pretty sure it was that older tan apartment building off Magnolia near the 91, the one with the tiny parking lot and the laundromat on the corner. I remember seeing the number 1842 on the front gate, but I didn’t catch the unit. I think he said he’s on the second floor facing the alley.

He was telling me again about the Riverside Urgent Care visit on Arlington Avenue back on January 18th. While we were in the exam room, he quietly told the PA he’s been self-medicating his wrist pain with leftover hydrocodone from that 2023 dental surgery at Riverside Dental Center, and that he sometimes downs 3–4 beers after work. The PA really warned him that combo could be dangerous.

Anyway, if you remember anything more specific about his place from when you picked him up that one time, let me know. I’d rather leave the address line blank than guess wrong.

Love,
Michael

artifacts.content:
Title: Meet w/ Daniel re: address & disability questions
Date: 2025-02-11 5:30 PM–6:15 PM
Location: Back table, Starbucks – 3380 La Sierra Ave, Riverside, CA 92503
Attendees: Michael Thompson; Daniel Ruiz

Notes:

Follow-up chat with Daniel the day after his Riverside Orthopedic & Sports Medicine appointment. He said Dr. Nguyen told him yesterday (Feb 10) that if the wrist doesn’t improve they might order an MRI and possibly a minor outpatient procedure. Doc mentioned this could limit his ability to keep working full-time for me at Thompson Garden & Landscaping Services and might even qualify him for partial disability through the state.

Agenda:
- Go over what the doctor actually wrote in his notes and what we need to pass along to the insurance adjuster.
- Clarify what address Daniel wants used on all the “injured person” sections. He mentioned he’s been staying with his cousin near University Avenue, somewhere around the 900 block, but his mail still goes to his old place off Magnolia. I need ONE address for the forms so they don’t kick anything back.
- Remind him to update his attorney at Martinez & Flores Law Group about the wrist situation, especially if they think the work limitations might affect his immigration paperwork or any future job stuff.
- Double-check that he understands I can’t list my business yard or my home address for him; it has to be a residence he actually uses.

Bring: copy of claim forms, pen, notepad.

metadata.title: Meet w/ Daniel re: address & disability questions
metadata.location: Starbucks – 3380 La Sierra Ave, Riverside, CA 92503

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Ruiz <d.ruiz1989@example.com>
To: Michael Thompson <mike@thompsongardenland.com>
Date: 2025-01-19 10:02 AM
Subject: My address for insurance papers

Hi Michael,

Thank you for taking me to Riverside Urgent Care yesterday and waiting with me. I appreciate your help and that you are doing the insurance claim.

For the forms where they ask for my address or the injured worker address, please do not use the Corona place from before. That is where I stayed back in 2023 when I had that fall on another job site and scraped my left knee. I never reported that one because I was scared it might affect the immigration paperwork and the adjustment-of-status application my lawyer at Martinez & Flores Law Group filed for me. That apartment in Corona was on Lincoln Avenue near the big shopping center, but I moved out a long time ago.

Right now I am renting a room in a small house near downtown Riverside, close to the intersection of Brockton Ave and 10th Street. The owner prefers not to have her full address on official things that don’t come directly from me, so I would rather bring you the exact number and zip code tomorrow in person so we don’t make mistakes.

If the insurance company really needs it by email, I will send it later today after I double-check the mailbox number.

Thanks again,
Daniel

metadata.subject: My address for insurance papers

artifacts.content:
Title: Drop off claim forms at Daniel’s place
Date: 2025-01-24 7:15 PM–7:45 PM
Location: Curb in front of 742 Brookside Avenue, Riverside, CA 92501
Attendees: Michael Thompson

Description:

Quick stop after work to hand-deliver the updated insurance claim packet to Daniel. He said the adjuster needs the pages where I filled in the “injured person address” and initialed the corrections. I wrote in the temporary mailing address he gave me over the phone (a PO box he’s using near Mission Inn Ave) but left his physical residence line blank until he’s 100% sure what he wants there.

He asked me not to pull into the driveway because there are too many cars, so plan to park on the street in front of 742 Brookside and text him when I arrive. That’s the house where he’s been crashing on and off, but he keeps saying it’s not his “real” address and that he might move again if the rent goes up.

Need to remind him to bring the discharge papers from Riverside Urgent Care on Arlington, especially the part where the PA wrote down that he’d been taking leftover hydrocodone from his 2023 dental surgery and mixing it with a few beers at night. The adjuster hinted they might ask about that, and I don’t want it to look like we hid anything.

Bring: envelope with claim forms, pen, sticky notes for any last-minute corrections.

metadata.title: Drop off claim forms at Daniel’s place

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <mike@thompsongardenland.com>
To: Karen Ellis <kellis@pacificclaimsadjusting.com>
Date: 2025-02-03 3:41 PM
Subject: Clarification on Daniel Ruiz’s address for claim #PC-11784

Hi Karen,

Following up on your voicemail about the missing "injured person address" on Daniel Ruiz’s paperwork for claim #PC-11784.

Daniel’s living situation has been a little unstable since before the November incident. He’s bounced between a room near downtown Riverside and staying with relatives closer to the university. For now, he asked that I list his mailing address as the PO Box he uses near Mission Inn Avenue, which I’ve already written in on the correspondence section of the form.

For the physical address line you highlighted, I didn’t want to guess. The last place I actually dropped him off was a beige duplex near the corner of Chicago Ave and Spruce Street, but I never saw the full number—only that the mailbox had “Unit A” on it. That’s why I left that section blank and put a sticky note asking you whether a mailing address alone would be acceptable until he settles on a permanent place.

At his February 10 follow-up at Riverside Orthopedic & Sports Medicine, Dr. Nguyen mentioned that if Daniel’s wrist doesn’t improve they may order an MRI and possibly do a minor outpatient procedure. The doctor also said this could limit Daniel’s ability to keep working full-time for me and might qualify him for partial disability through the state, which I know may affect how you handle his claim, but it doesn’t change the fact that I don’t have a reliable street address for him yet.

Please let me know how you’d like me to proceed.

Best,
Michael Thompson
Owner, Thompson Garden & Landscaping Services
